gauge.name: Suwannee River at Ellaville - US 90 (in NAVD88)

historic.statement: Stage is in MSL. Datum of 27.22 added.

impacts.statement: Water reaches the top of the Interstate 10 bridge railing.
Water reaches the top of the US-90 bridge railing.
Water reaches the Interstate 10 bridge travel lanes.
Water reaches the US-90 bridge travel lanes.
Water reaches the approach road surface of the Interstate 10 bridges in Madison County.
Water reaches the base of the US-90 bridge.
Water reaches the base of the Interstate 10 bridge across the Suwannee River.
In Suwannee County, River Road floods just south of Interstate 10 at 209th Road.
In Suwannee County, several homes off River Road in Ellaville begin to flood.
Electric substation in Suwannee County floods.
Water reaches the approach road surface to the US-90 bridge in Suwannee County about one quarter mile east of the bridge. Water begins to surround the electric substation in Suwannee County.
SE 165th Street in Madison County floods on the north side of Interstate 10.
Residences on 38th Terrace flood in Suwannee County.
Water reaches the approach road surface to the US-90 bridge in Madison County about 0.4 miles west of the bridge.
Water approaches two homes off 38th Terrace in Suwannee County.
Access to Suwannee River State Park becomes restricted in Suwannee County.
205th Court and 205th Terrace in Suwannee County begin to flood.
Extensive lowland flooding occurs on the Madison and Suwannee sides of the river, including in the Suwannee River State Park.
Suwannee River campsites begin to flood in Hamilton and Suwannee Counties. Picnic areas in Madison County near the old US-90 bridge begin to flood.
Florida Fish and Wildlife Commission begins enforcement of a no wake zone on the Suwannee River from the US-90 bridge at Ellaville south to the SR-51 bridge at Luraville.

dataAttribution.title: Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
Flood stages and impact descriptions for this site are referenced to the NAVD88 datum. Additional datum information can be found at the bottom of this page.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

dataAttribution.title: Observations courtesy of the US Geological Survey

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
